description: Aliciella latifolia, also known as broad-leaved gilia, is a foul smelling annual plant in the Phlox family (Polemoniaceae) found in deserts of the southwestern United States.
origin: NORTHERN AMERICA, MXN BC, UTA OO, ARI, MXN
synonyms: ["Navarretia latifolia", "Gilia latifolia"]

care.tips: Native to: NEV, MXN BS, ARI OO, UTA, MXN SI

origin: WAS, MXN SO, MEXICO, UTA, ORE OO
synonyms: ["Gilia gilioides", "Navarretia gilioides", "Collomia gilioides", "Microsteris gilioides"]

care.tips: Native to: WAS, MXN SO, MEXICO, UTA, ORE OO

description: Cobaea scandens, the cup-and-saucer vine, cathedral bells, Mexican ivy, or monastery bells, is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family Polemoniaceae. It is native to Mexico, with isolated sightings elsewhere in tropical central and South America.
origin: Mexico
synonyms: ["Cobaea scandens alba", "Cobaea lasseri", "Cobaea scandens var. foliis-variegatis", "Cobaea scandens variegata", "Rosenbergia scandens"]

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: seasonally dry tropical
care.tips: Similar plants: Bougainvillea glabra | Native to: ECU, MDG OO, ASIA TROPICAL, NORTHERN SOUTH AMERICA, CARIBBEAN
common_pests: May attract some aphids and spider mites.

description: Collomia grandiflora is a western North American annual plant in the phlox family (Polemoniaceae), known by the common names grand collomia, large-flowered mountain trumpet, and large-flowered collomia. It usually appears in sandy habitats and is cultivated as an ornamental.
origin: UTA, AGS, ARI OO, CAL, AGS SC
synonyms: ["Collomia grandiflora var. tenuiflora", "Collomia grandiflora f. tenuiflora", "Gilia grandiflora", "Collomia scabra", "Collomia grandiflora subsp. diffusa", "Collomia grandiflora f. diffusa", "Collomia grandiflora f. scabra", "Collomia grandiflora f. axillaris", "Gilia grandiflora var. axillaris", "Collomia grandiflora var. axillaris"]

care.tips: Native to: NEV, IDA OO, WYO OO, SOUTHWESTERN EUROPE, COL OO

description: Collomia heterophylla is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name variableleaf collomia. It is native to western North America from British Columbia to Idaho to central California, where it grows in several types of habitat, including open areas on gravelly mountain slopes. It is a hairy annual herb producing a branching, erect stem.
origin: WAS OO, NORTHWESTERN USA, ORE OO, CAL, WESTERN CANADA
synonyms: ["Navarretia heterophylla", "Courtoisia daucifolia", "Collomia heterophylla f. daucifolia", "Gilia heterophylla", "Gilia sessei"]

care.tips: Native to: WESTERN CANADA, WAS OO, BRC OO, IDA OO, BRC

description: Collomia linearis is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common names tiny trumpet and slenderleaf collomia. This tiny wildflower is native to North America, where it is fairly widespread across the north and west. It is an annual herb, rarely exceeding 30 centimetres (12 in) in height, with a velvety erect stem bearing long, narrow green leaves. Atop the stem is a cluster of up to 20 white or light pink flowers, each about a centimeter across.
origin: EASTERN CANADA, NORTHEASTERN USA, KRA, RUC OO, NWH
synonyms: ["Navarretia linearis", "Collomia parviflora f. picta", "Gilia linearis", "Collomia linearis var. picta", "Collomia tinctoria f. subulata", "Collomia parviflora f. humilis", "Collomia linearis var. subulata", "Collomia linearis var. boulderensis", "Collomia parviflora f. lanceolata", "Gilia linearis var. subulata"]

care.tips: Native to: NEV, INI, ARI, YUK OO, NBR

description: Eriastrum densifolium is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name giant woollystar. This wildflower is native to California and Baja California where it grows in open areas such as sand dunes and dry washes. It grows on an erect stem with slightly hairy to densely woolly foliage, often giving the plant a dark gray-green color. The leaves are narrow and spike-shaped with pointed lobes.
origin: MXN, MXN SO, CAL, MXN BS, MIDDLE EUROPE
synonyms: ["Navarretia densifolia subsp. eudensifolia", "Navarretia densifolia", "Welwitschia densifolia", "Gilia densifolia", "Gilia hugelia", "Hugelia densifolia"]

description: Eriastrum diffusum is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name miniature woollystar. It is native to the southwestern United States from California to Texas, where it grows in many types of open habitat. This is an annual herb producing a thin, usually woolly stem up to about 20 centimeters long, growing erect or spreading outward. The leaves are divided into 2 to 4 narrow, threadlike linear lobes.

Eriastrum eremicum, the desert woollystar, is an annual plant in the phlox family (Polemoniaceae) found in the Sonoran Desert. It often grows in great abundance covering the desert floor in a blanket of sky blue color.
origin: MXN, MXN SO, UTA, NEV OO, ARI OO
synonyms: ["Hugelia eremica", "Gilia eremica"]

description: Eriastrum sapphirinum is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name sapphire woollystar. This wildflower is endemic to California where it is found in many habitats throughout the state. It is an annual reaching anywhere from 5 to 40 centimeters in height, forming clumps or singular spindly stems. The stem is erect and may be reddish to green, and has the occasional threadlike leaf with a texture ranging from sparse hairs to a coat of dense wool.
origin: MEXICO, MXN BC, NORTHERN AMERICA, MXN BS, CAL OO
synonyms: ["Navarretia virgata var. sapphirina", "Gilia virgata var. sapphirina", "Hugelia virgata var. sapphirina", "Gilia sapphirina"]

description: Eriastrum wilcoxii is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name Wilcox's woollystar. It is native to the western United States, where it grows in various types of desert and plateau habitat. It is an annual herb producing a thin, branching, usually woolly stem up to about 30 centimeters tall. The leaves are narrow and thready, up to 3 centimeters long, and sometimes divided into two or more lobes.

The annual flowering plant Gilia achilleifolia is known commonly as California gilia, California gily-flower, and blue gilia. It is native to California but grows in other areas of North America where it has been introduced.
origin: MEXICO, BLT LI, ASK OO, MXN BC, VER OO
synonyms: ["Navarretia achilleifolia"]

description: Gilia angelensis is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name chaparral gilia. It is native to the coastal hills and mountains of California and Baja California, where it is a member of the chaparral ecosystem., especially in the Transverse Ranges.

Gilia cana is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name showy gilia. It is native to California and Nevada where it grows in open areas with gravelly and sandy soils, such as desert and rocky slopes. The Mojave Desert range of some subspecies may extend into Arizona.
Gilia capitata is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common names blue-thimble-flower, bluehead gilia, blue field gilia, and globe gilia.
origin: Alaska south to western U.S.A
synonyms: ["Navarretia capitata"]

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: temperate

description: Gilia latiflora is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common names hollyleaf gilia and broad-flowered gilia. It is endemic to deserts and mountains of southern California and the adjacent margin of Nevada.
origin: SOUTHWESTERN USA, NORTHERN AMERICA, CAL OO, CAL
synonyms: ["Gilia tenuiflora subsp. latiflora", "Gilia tenuiflora var. latiflora", "Navarretia latiflora"]

description: Gilia sinuata is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common names rosy gilia and rosy phlox. It is native to western North America from British Columbia to New Mexico, where it can be found in a number of open habitats, generally in sandy soil, and at elevations from 500 to 7500 feet.
origin: NEV, MEXICO, MXN BC, NORTHWESTERN USA, MXN BS
synonyms: ["Gilia tenuiflora var. sinuata", "Gilia inconspicua var. sinuata", "Gilia inconspicua subsp. sinuata"]

description: Gilia stellata is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name star gilia. It is native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico, where it is a common resident of desert washes and sandy mountain slopes.
Gilia tricolor is an annual flowering plant in the phlox family (Polemoniaceae).
origin: MAS OO, RUS OO, MIDDLE EUROPE, EASTERN EUROPE, MAS
synonyms: ["Navarretia tricolor"]

origin: MXG, MXE AG, NWM OO, MXE HI, MXE SL
synonyms: ["Polemonium incisum", "Navarretia incisa", "Gilia incisa", "Gilia lindheimeriana", "Gilia perennans"]

description: Ipomopsis aggregata is a species of biennial flowering plant in the phlox family, Polemoniaceae, commonly known as scarlet trumpet, scarlet gilia, or skyrocket because of its scarlet red flowers with lobes curving back as if blown back by rocketing through the air.
origin: MEXICO, NEV, MXN BC, MXN BS, NORTH CENTRAL USA
synonyms: ["Callisteris aggregata", "Navarretia aggregata", "Gilia aggregata", "Batanthes aggregata", "Cantua aggregata", "Collomia aggregata", "Ipomeria aggregata", "Gilia aggregata subsp. euaggregata"]

description: Ipomopsis congesta is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family, known by the common name ballhead ipomopsis. It is native to much of western North America, where it grows in many habitats from alpine peaks to low-elevation scrub. It is a perennial herb which varies in appearance, especially across subspecies and climates. It may take the form of a squat patch with stems under 10 centimeters in height or a more erect form up to 30 centimeters tall. The stems are often hairy to woolly.
origin: UTA, NWM OO, ARI OO, CAL, ARI
synonyms: ["Navarretia congesta", "Gilia congesta"]

description: Ipomopsis longiflora, commonly known as flaxflowered gilia or flaxflowered ipomopsis, is a plant species. The Zuni people utilize the dried, powdered flowers and water of I. longiflora subsp. longiflora to prepare a poultice for removing hair from newborns and children.
origin: MXN, MXN SO, MXE AG, UTA, NWM OO
synonyms: ["Navarretia longiflora", "Cantua longiflora", "Gilia longiflora", "Collomia longiflora", "Batanthes longiflora"]

description: Ipomopsis rubra is a flowering plant of the phlox family native to North America in the state of Texas and the southeastern United States, commonly known as standing cypress, scarlet gilia, Texas plume, flame flower, and indian spur. This classification is synonymous with Gilia rubra. This flower is noteworthy for its bright, upturned flowers.
origin: Southeastern United States
synonyms: ["Polemonium rubrum", "Cantua coronopifolia", "Ipomeria coronopifolia", "Cantua thyrsoidea", "Ipomopsis elegans", "Gilia coronopifolia", "Cantua floridana", "Gilia floridana", "Gilia rubra", "Gilia rubra var. capillacea"]

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day) | Climate: temperate

description: Langloisia is a genus of flowering plants in the family Polemoniaceae. It is monotypic, being represented by the single species Langloisia setosissima, also known as bristly langloisia, bristly-calico, Great Basin langloisia, and lilac sunbonnets. It is native to the western United States and north-western Mexico, where it is found in desert washes and on rocky slopes and plains from eastern Oregon and Idaho, south via Nevada and Utah to eastern California and Arizona.
Leptosiphon androsaceus is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name of false babystars. It is an annual herb that is endemic to California.
origin: NORTHERN AMERICA, PEN, CAL OO, SOUTHWESTERN USA, PEN OO
synonyms: ["Gilia androsacea var. detonsa", "Gilia androsacea subsp. euandrosacea", "Leptosiphon mariposianus", "Linanthus longitubus", "Gilia androsacea subsp. montana", "Gilia longituba var. rosacea", "Gilia androsacea subsp. serrulata", "Gilia micrantha f. longituba", "Leptosiphon parviflorus", "Navarretia androsacea"]

origin: NEV, MEXICO, MXN BC, MXN BS, CAL OO
synonyms: ["Leptosiphon aureus", "Navarretia aurea", "Gilia lutea var. aurea", "Dactylophyllum aureum", "Gilia aurea", "Gilia micrantha f. aurea", "Linanthus aureus"]

description: Leptosiphon ciliatus is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name whiskerbrush.
origin: NEV, NORTHERN AMERICA, NORTHWESTERN USA, CAL OO, SOUTHWESTERN USA
synonyms: ["Gilia ciliata", "Navarretia ciliata", "Linanthus ciliatus"]

description: Leptosiphon liniflorus is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name narrowflower flaxflower.
origin: MXN, MXN SO, WAS OO, NORTHWESTERN USA, NEV OO
synonyms: ["Gilia liniflora f. pharnaceoides", "Navarretia liniflora", "Gilia liniflora subsp. euliniflora", "Gilia liniflora", "Dactylophyllum liniflorum", "Linanthus liniflorus var. vallicola", "Gilia liniflora var. filipes", "Linanthus liniflorus subsp. pharnaceoides", "Linanthus liniflorus var. pharnaceoides", "Gilia pharnaceoides"]

description: Leptosiphon nuttallii is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name Nuttall's linanthus.
origin: NEV, MEXICO, MXN BC, NORTHWESTERN USA, MXN BS
synonyms: ["Linanthus nuttallii", "Navarretia nuttallii", "Gilia nuttallii", "Linanthastrum nuttallii", "Siphonella nuttallii"]

description: Leptosiphon parviflorus is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name variable linanthus.
origin: MXN, MXN SO, SOUTHWESTERN USA, MXN SI, MEXICO
synonyms: ["Leptosiphon luteus aureus", "Linanthus parviflorus", "Linanthus parviflorus var. croceus", "Leptosiphon roseus", "Linanthus parviflorus var. luteolus", "Leptosiphon hybridus", "Leptosiphon aureus", "Linanthus androsaceus subsp. laetus", "Gilia lutea var. rosea", "Gilia lutea"]

origin: SOUTHWESTERN USA, NORTHERN AMERICA, CAL OO, CAL
synonyms: ["Gilia californica", "Leptodactylon californicum", "Navarretia californica"]

description: Linanthus dianthiflorus is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common names fringed linanthus and ground pink.
origin: MXN, MXN SO, MXN SI, SOUTHWESTERN USA, MEXICO
synonyms: ["Gilia dianthiflora", "Fenzlia dianthiflora", "Navarretia dianthiflora", "Fenzlia speciosa", "Fenzlia concinna", "Gilia dianthoides", "Gilia fenzlia", "Gilia dianthoides var. farinosa", "Linanthus dianthiflorus subsp. farinosus", "Gilia dianthoides var. alba"]

description: Linanthus dichotomus is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name evening-snow. It is native to western North America, including most of the southwestern United States, where it is a common member of the flora in a number of habitat types. It is often found on the serpentine soils of California. This is an annual herb producing several thin, waxy, erect stems up to 20 centimeters tall. The leaves are divided into linear lobes 1 or 2 centimeters long.
origin: MXN, MXN SO, UTA, NEV OO, ARI OO
synonyms: ["Navarretia dichotoma", "Gilia dichotoma", "Gilia linanthus"]

description: Linanthus pungens is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common names granite prickly-phlox and granite gilia. It is native to western North America from British Columbia to Baja California and east to Montana and New Mexico.
origin: MEXICO, NEV, MXN BC, MRY, NORTHWESTERN USA
synonyms: ["Leptodactylon pungens", "Cantua pungens", "Batanthes pungens", "Navarretia pungens", "Gilia pungens", "Leptodactylon pungens var. eupungens", "Gilia pungens subsp. eupungens"]

origin: MEXICO, MXN BC, NIC, MXN BS, MXC TL
synonyms: ["Cantua glandulosa", "Hoitzia glandulosa"]

origin: MEXICO, MXE ZA, MXN BC, MXN BS, MXN SO
synonyms: ["Cantua coccinea", "Phlox spinosilla", "Hoitzia coccinea", "Hoitzia mexicana", "Loeselia mexicana var. lutea", "Loeselia coccinea", "Cantua hoitzia"]

description: Loeseliastrum matthewsii is an annual herbaceous plant of the Polemoniaceae family known by the common name desert calico. It is native to the Mojave and Sonoran Deserts of western North America, where it is relatively common. It is a small plant with alternately arranged leaves, each up to 4 centimeters long and edged with bristle-tipped teeth. The flower is white, lavender, and purple streaked with a maroon arch over a white patch on each of the upper lobes.
Navarretia hamata is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name hooked pincushionplant.
Navarretia intertexta is a species of flowering plant in the family phlox, known by the common name needleleaf pincushionplant.

Navarretia leucocephala is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family known by the common name whitehead pincushionplant, or whitehead navarretia. It is native to North America, including much of the western United States and central Canada. It generally grows in wet or moist terrestrial habitat such as vernal pools.

Navarretia squarrosa is a spreading annual plant from North America which is noted for its skunk-like odour. It grows to between 10 and 60 cm in height and has tubular lilac pink to deep blue flowers up to 12 mm in diameter in dense terminal heads, encircled by spiny sepals and bracts. The leaves are pinnately lobed and spiny.
All species have five sepals, five petals fused, and five stamens that alternate with the lobes of the corolla.
origin: North America

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: All species have five sepals, five petals fused, and five stamens that alternate with the lobes of the corolla.
origin: North America

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Phlox bifida, commonly known as cleft phlox or sand phlox, is a perennial herbaceous plant in the Polemoniaceae (phlox) family that is native to the central United States.

Phlox carolina, the thickleaf phlox, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. It is an herbaceous perennial growing to 1.2 m (4 ft) tall by 45 cm (18 in) wide, with leaves to 13 cm (5 in) long, and purple or pink flowers in summer.
The specific epithet carolina refers to its native habitat in the eastern United States. It grows in woodland edges and openings. Flowers attract bees, hummingbirds, and butterflies.
origin: East Central and Southeastern USA

care.light_guide: NC State: Dappled Sunlight (Shade through upper canopy all day)

description: All species have five sepals, five petals fused, and five stamens that alternate with the lobes of the corolla.
origin: North America

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Phlox divaricata, the wild blue phlox, woodland phlox, or wild sweet william, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae, native to forests and fields in eastern North America.
origin: Eastern North America

care.light_guide: NC State: Dappled Sunlight (Shade through upper canopy all day)

description: Phlox drummondii is a flowering plant in the genus Phlox of the family Polemoniaceae. Native to Texas, it is also widely distributed in the southeastern United States, especially along public highways. P. drummondii is often used as an ornamental plant. The flowers have a wide range of colours "from white and cream through pinks, lilacs, roses, purples and reds, to almost black".
origin: Texas

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Phlox glaberrima, commonly called smooth phlox and sometimes marsh phlox, is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family. It is native to the Midwestern and Southeastern United States where it is found in moist to wet areas. It can be found in both prairies and forests, where it is an indicator of high quality habitat.
origin: Central and Southeastern  U.S.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Microsteris is a monotypic genus of flowering plants in the phlox family containing the single species Microsteris gracilis, known by the common name slender phlox.
All species have five sepals, five petals fused, and five stamens that alternate with the lobes of the corolla.
origin: North America

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: All species have five sepals, five petals fused, and five stamens that alternate with the lobes of the corolla.
origin: North America

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Phlox maculata, commonly called meadow phlox, as well as wild sweet William and marsh phlox, is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae, native the eastern United States and introduced to eastern Canada. It is a perennial.
origin: Central and eastern U.S.A.

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
common_problems: This phlox has better mildew resistance than P.
paniculata but it needs good air circulation and overhead watering should be avoided.
Root rot and spider mites can be an issue.
common_pests: Root rot and spider mites can be an issue.
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Infusion of root used as a wash by the Cherokees to make children grow and fatten.

description: Phlox paniculata is a species of flowering plant in the phlox family (Polemoniaceae). It is native to parts of the eastern and central United States. It is extensively cultivated in temperate regions as an ornamental plant and has become established in the wild in scattered locales in other regions. Common names include fall phlox, garden phlox, perennial phlox, summer phlox, and panicled phlox.
origin: Eastern United States

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Leaf extract has been used as a laxative and for treating boils.

tr: Bahçe Alev Çiçeği, Bahçe Floksu, Yaz Floksu

description: Phlox pilosa, the downy phlox or prairie phlox, is an herbaceous plant in the family Polemoniaceae. It is native to eastern North America, where it is found in open areas such as prairies and woodlands.
origin: North America

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)
common_problems: Downy phlox has no serious insect or disease problems although hot, dry conditions can promote spider mite infestations.
It has resistance to powdery mildew.
common_pests: Downy phlox has no serious insect or disease problems although hot, dry conditions can promote spider mite infestations.
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Meskwaki Indians used an infusion of leaves to cure and purify the blood, and as a wash for eczema. They also used a compound containing the root used as a 'love medicine'.

description: All species have five sepals, five petals fused, and five stamens that alternate with the lobes of the corolla.
origin: North America

care.light_guide: NC State: Full sun (6 or more hours of direct sunlight a day)

description: Phlox stolonifera is a species of flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. It is a perennial herbaceous plant that is native to the eastern United States. It occurs in woodlands and stream banks in the vicinity of the Appalachian Mountains from Pennsylvania south to northern Georgia. Naturalized populations occur as far north as Québec, Canada.
origin: Appalachian mountains

care.light_guide: NC State: Deep shade (Less than 2 hours to no direct sunlight)
care.used_for_details: (NC State ethnobotany) Rock gardens, cottage gardens, borders, edges, and ground cover

description: The flowers are replaced by an inconspicuous oval seed capsule. The seed capsule is 3-valved, 4 mm long, and contains 3 or more seeds. It spreads by reseeding.
origin: Central and Eastern Canada and Central and Eastern United States

care.light_guide: NC State: Dappled Sunlight (Shade through upper canopy all day)
care.toxicity_note: Non-toxic to Dogs, Non-Toxic to Cats, Non-Toxic to Horses.
common_problems: Moss phlox is relatively pest and disease-free and has moderate drought tolerance.
It is resistant to powdery mildew, unlike most other species of phlox.
Rabbits may feed on the foliage, but it is less palatable to deer.
Some cultivars have shown good resistance to phytophthora and southern blight.
common_pests: Moss phlox is relatively pest and disease-free and has moderate drought tolerance.
Spider mites can be a problem in hot, dry conditions, and nematodes can cause damage to the leaves in wet and humid conditions.

tr: Dağ Alev Çiçeği, Yosun Floksu, Dağ Floksu

description: Polemonium acutiflorum, known as tall Jacob's-ladder, is a flowering plant in the family Polemoniaceae. It is native to western Canada and Alaska.
The flowers are adorned with a yellow halo and a white eye.
origin: High Arctic
